title: adenia gracilis subsp. pinnata
intro: Adenia gracilis subsp. pinnata, also known as the African potato or elephant's foot, is a plant species in the family Passifloraceae. The plant's roots and tubers are rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Adenia gracilis subsp. pinnata include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In traditional medicine, the plant's roots and tubers are often prepared as a decoction or infusion for consumption, and may also be used in the form of a paste or ointment for topical application.
